title: List of Colleges Accepting 160-200 Cutoff in TNEA

detail.title: List of Colleges Accepting 160-200 Cutoff in TNEA
detail.short_info: TNEA Engineering Colleges for Cutoff Marks Between 160 to 200 in Tamil Nadu: Check list of top colleges, branch-wise options, previous year cutoffs, and complete counselling process for 2026 admissions.

content.text: Students scoring 160 to 200 cut off marks (out of 200) in TNEA have excellent opportunities to secure seats in top government, government-aided, and reputed private engineering colleges across Tamil Nadu. This range is considered very good and opens doors to competitive branches like CSE, ECE, IT, Mechanical, and more in well-known institutions. This article provides a complete list of colleges, branch-wise options, previous year trends, counselling process, and expert tips for TNEA 2026 admissions.

sections.heading: TNEA Colleges for Cutoff Marks Between 160 to 200 – Overview Table

rows: Top Govt, Govt Aided & Reputed Private
Good in CSE, ECE, IT, EEE, Mechanical
High-achieving students aiming for top colleges

sections.heading: List of Colleges Accepting 160-200 Cutoff in TNEA

content.text: Candidates in this range can target the following reputed colleges:

rows: University Departments of Anna University, MIT Campus, Chromepet, Tambaram Taluk
Sri Sivasubramaniya Nadar College of Engineering (Autonomous), Kalavakkam, OMR
University Departments of Anna University, CEG Campus, Guindy
PSG Institute of Technology and Applied Research, Avinashi Road, Neelambur
Coimbatore Institute of Technology (Autonomous), Civil Aerodrome Post
Chennai Institute of Technology (Autonomous), Puduper Village, Nandambakkam Post, Kundrathur
Sri Venkateswara College of Engineering, Sriperumbudur
St. Joseph’s College of Engineering (Autonomous), OMR

items: 190+: CSE, ECE, IT in Top Tier Colleges (Anna University, SSN, PSG)
180-190: Good branches in KCT, TCE, CIT, SVCE
170-180: Most branches including Mechanical, EEE, Civil in reputed colleges
160-170: Strong options in good private colleges with decent placements
